methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Built Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Noah Ready-Campbell and Andrew Liang, has raised $112M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates two methods across a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, all operating in Robotics & Manufacturing.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $17M total-raised proxy, implying a valuation of $29M. Peer-median methodology examines five same-sector comparables at Series B, C, and D stages—Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C)—yielding a median valuation of $1.6B with a range of $1.0B to $2.0B.

The two methods produce a wide triangulated range: $25M (low), $839M (median), and $1.9B (high). The stage-progression method significantly underweights the peer median, likely because the $17M proxy underrepresents capital deployed in a frontier-robotics context where later-stage peers command substantial valuations. The peer median of $1.6B aligns with Hadrian, a direct Series C comparable. Key uncertainties include revenue maturity, deployment scale, and the degree to which automation-focused capital deployment justifies premium multiples relative to traditional manufacturing tech. The analysis supports a valuation range of $800M–$1.6B, anchored to peer stage and sector precedent.
